channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 4 ChatGPT ‘Custom Instructions’ that’ll cut your busywork in half
item.description: If you’re still copy-pasting the same formatting requests or resorting to “write this like a professional” prompts every single time you open ChatGPT, you’re working too hard. There’s an essential feature buried in the Settings menu called Custom Instructions.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Unitree G1 humanoid robot ice skates and Rollerblades
item.description: Watch Unitree's G1 humanoid robot glide on Rollerblades and ice skates, pulling off spins and flips while staying perfectly balanced in real time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Are we ready to give AI agents the keys to the cloud? Cloudflare thinks so
item.description: Cloudflare is giving AI agents full autonomy to spin up new apps. Starting today, agents working on behalf of humans can create a Cloudflare account, begin a paid subscription, register a domain, and then receive an API token to let them immediately deploy code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk spars with OpenAI attorney in trial over company's evolution from a nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk on Thursday sparred with an attorney for OpenAI during his third day of testimony in the contentious trial over the company’s pivot from nonprofit status to a for-profit venture valued at hundreds of billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple reportedly abandons Vision Pro
item.description: It was only this month that incoming Apple CEO John Ternus said of the Vision Pro, “I think we’re still very much in the early innings of spatial computing. We’re super excited about it.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI venture funding to shoot up this year as bubble looms
item.description: Venture funding of AI companies in 2026 will easily smash funding records set in 2025, with some heavy deals already consummated in the first quarter, according to market researchers. Data from Crunchbase shows that $300 billion poured into 6,000 startups worldwide during the first quarter of 2026.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
